workflow.name: Summarize Gmail into Notion with OpenAI and create audio digests in Google Drive
workflow.description:
**Gmail → AI Summary → Notion + Audio Digest**

This n8n workflow turns incoming Gmail emails into structured AI summaries and optional audio digests, automatically delivered to Notion and Google Drive.

It is designed to reduce email overload by transforming raw messages into concise, readable, and listenable content.

What this workflow does

On a scheduled basis, the workflow:

- Retrieves Gmail messages (all subjects or filtered)

- Marks processed emails as read to avoid duplicates

- Extracts and normalizes the email body

- Uses OpenAI to generate a clean, structured summary

From the summary, the workflow branches into two outputs:

Text summary

The final AI-generated summary is appended as a block in Notion

Ideal for daily logs, knowledge bases, or team dashboards

Audio transcript (optional)

- The summary text is converted into speech using a TTS model

- The audio file is uploaded to Google Drive

- A shareable link is generated

- The audio reference is added back into Notion

Key features

- Automated Gmail ingestion

- AI-powered email summarization

- JavaScript preprocessing for clean input

- Notion integration for structured storage

- Text-to-speech audio generation

- Google Drive hosting for audio files

- Error-aware branching for TTS generation

- Idempotent and schedule-safe execution

*Typical use cases*

- Daily or weekly email digests

- Executive summaries of inbox activity

- Audio briefings you can listen to on the go

- Knowledge capture from important emails

- Reducing cognitive load from long email threads

Who this template is for

- Professionals dealing with high email volume

- Teams using Notion as a central workspace

- n8n users building AI productivity automations

- Anyone who wants emails summarized instead of skimmed

This template is designed to be practical, extensible, and production-ready, and can be easily adapted to:

- multiple Gmail labels

- different summary styles

- alternative TTS providers

- additional destinations (Slack, Docs, databases)

workflow.name: Generate a summary from Gmail and upload it into Notion

parameters.text:
=Act as a personal editor. Analyze the text of the provided emails ({{ $json.text }}) and create a concise summary of the key news or information.

Formatting rules for Notion:

Use UPPERCASE for section titles (DO NOT use # or ##).

Use bullet points (-) for details.

Avoid complex markdown symbols such as tables or code blocks.

Be brief and direct.

The final result must be a maximum of 1600 characters.

At the beginning of the summary, include (only once) the date (the Monday of the week you are considering).

Exclude events from the summary.

=You are the editor-in-chief of a weekly newsletter.
Below you will find a series of raw summaries from various emails received this week.
Your task is to create a SINGLE final narrative text (Weekly Recap) that merges the most important news.

CORE RULES:

The final text MUST be under 1500 characters (including spaces). Be concise.

Use a conversational yet structured style (use bullet points if needed).

Do not use Markdown formatting (UPPERCASE for titles, bold for emphasis).

Ignore emails that appear to be spam or not important if you need to save space.

Start with a title: "WEEKLY RECAP:" and add the date of the Monday of the relevant week.

Here are the raw summaries:
{{ $json.final_summary }}

parameters.content:
## Gmail flow 
Retrieve emails with AI subjects.
Tag as READ

## Audio transcript
**Generate an audio transcript and upload to Google Drive

nodes.name: Create a link to audio file in Notion

parameters.content:
## How it works

This workflow automates the entire journey from inbox noise to a clean, shareable audio summary. It starts with a weekly trigger that scans Gmail for emails matching specific subjects. Each message is fetched, marked as read, and its body is extracted and normalized to avoid formatting chaos.

The cleaned content is then passed to an OpenAI Chat Model, which generates a concise written summary. A short JavaScript step acts as glue logic: it aggregates content, enforces structure, and prepares the final prompt. A second OpenAI step produces the polished “Final Summary,” designed to be readable and narration-friendly.

That summary is saved directly into Notion as a text block, creating a permanent written log. From there, the flow branches into audio mode: the text is converted to speech, an audio file is generated, and the file is uploaded to Google Drive. Permissions are applied automatically, a public URL is created, and that link is appended back into the same Notion page. Result: text + audio, always paired, always traceable.

### Setup steps

- Configure the Gmail node with subject filters and enable “mark as read.”

- Connect OpenAI credentials for both summarization and text-to-speech.

- Adjust the JavaScript node only if you want different aggregation rules.

- Set the Notion database/page where summaries should be appended.

- Configure Google Drive upload, sharing, and link creation.

- Run once manually to validate permissions, then let the weekly trigger do its thing.

Compact, deterministic, and low-maintenance—this flow quietly turns emails into knowledge you can read or listen to.

## Create summary text

Summarize with LLM

main.node: Create a link to audio file in Notion

user.name: Alberto Idrio
user.bio:
I design and build production-ready automation workflows with n8n, focusing on API integrations, LLM orchestration, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems.
My work combines practical automation, clean architecture, and real-world AI use cases—always aiming for workflows that are robust, maintainable, and easy to extend.

I’m especially interested in document pipelines, vector databases, and turning complex systems into simple, reliable automations.
